frameworks.name: ideal observer / optimal serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Formal model predicting reliability-weighted integration of past with current stimulus.

frameworks.name: temporal redundancy exploitation
frameworks.one_line: Brain leverages natural-scene serial correlations as optimization strategy.

core_question: Does serial dependence in perception confer a functional advantage predicted by an optimal-integration model?
key_assumptions: Natural scenes exhibit strong moment-to-moment serial correlations
Observer aims to minimize reproduction errors
Past stimuli should be weighted by current stimulus reliability
manipulations: Current stimulus reliability (e.g. low spatial frequency oblique Gabors)
key_findings: Serial dependence scales with current stimulus reliability, strongest for less precise stimuli
Serial dependence depends on similarity between successive stimuli
Serial dependence leads to faster response times
interpretations: Serial dependence behaves as predicted by an ideal observer minimizing reproduction errors
Benefits of serial integration extend beyond reproduction accuracy to response speed
